Container.Name: Pad
Container.Overview:
Pad is local-first project management for developers and AI coding agents. Single Go binary, SQLite by default, MCP-ready (Claude Code / Cursor / Windsurf). Your data stays on your Unraid box — no telemetry, no account required, no cloud lock-in.
After install, click the container's [b]Logs[/b] button to grab the first-run bootstrap token, then visit the WebUI to claim the first admin. See the install guide for screenshots: https://getpad.dev/docs/self-hosting/unraid
Container.Description: Pad is local-first project management for developers and AI coding agents. Built for the homelab — single binary, SQLite, no telemetry. Your data stays on your Unraid box.

Config.Name: WebUI Port
Config.Description: Pad web UI. Browse to http://[your-tower]:[port]/ after install.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Pad's persistent data: SQLite database, attachments, encryption key, logs, and config. Back this up to keep your workspace safe.

Config.Name: PUID
Config.Description: User ID the pad process runs as. Default 99 (nobody) matches Unraid's appdata permissions. Change only if you know what you're doing — must be a positive integer.

Config.Name: PGID
Config.Description: Group ID the pad process runs as. Default 100 (users) matches Unraid's appdata permissions. Change only if you know what you're doing — must be a positive integer.

Config.Name: Log Level
Config.Description: Verbosity for the server log. One of: debug, info, warn, error.

Config.Name: Public URL
Config.Description: External URL when pad is behind a reverse proxy (SWAG / NGINX Proxy Manager / etc.). Required for emailed invitation links to point at the right host. Example: https://pad.example.com

Config.Name: Maileroo API Key
Config.Description: Optional. Enables transactional email (workspace invitations). Without it, invites use copyable join codes via the CLI. Sign up at maileroo.com.

Config.Name: Email From
Config.Description: Sender address for invitation emails. Required if Maileroo API Key is set. Must be on a domain you control.

Config.Name: Email From Name
Config.Description: Display name on outbound emails (the human-friendly part before the address). Defaults to 'Pad'.
